title: AWIA Risk and Resilience Assessment (RRA) currency — evidence the 5-year cycle and population-tier deadlines are met
exclusions: Excludes Community Water Systems (CWS) serving ≤3,300 people which are not subject to s1433. Excludes wastewater-only systems (Section 1433 applies to CWS providing drinking water; wastewater systems are governed separately under CWA s1450 / UC-22.53.5). Excludes systems in EPA-granted extension windows documented in `awia_extension_lookup`.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.53.1` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `awia_clause="AWIA-s1433a"`. Every row pins (pwsid, system_name, population_served, tier, rra_completed_at, rra_certification_submitted_at, next_rra_due_at, days_to_due, certification_letter_url, rra_status). Dashboard `AWIA — RRA Currency` surfaces each Public Water System (PWS) and its 5-year cycle.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Continuously evidences (a) every CWS with population >3,300 has a current RRA, (b) RRA was completed by the original 2019–2020 tiered deadlines, (c) the EPA certification was submitted, and (d) the 5-year update cycle is tracked. This is the full scope of the s1433(a) duty.
compliance.controlObjective: Proves continuous compliance with the Safe Drinking Water Act s1433(a) RRA duty.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-22.53.1` archived to `audit_evidence`, the `AWIA — RRA Currency` dashboard, and per-system certification-letter URLs (EPA AWIA Reporting Portal submissions).

controlTest.positiveScenario: Set a CWS's `rra_completed_at` to 5 years and 2 months ago. UC-22.53.1 MUST flag `rra_status="overdue"`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Complete a fresh RRA, submit the EPA certification, and update the lookup. UC-22.53.1 MUST record `rra_status="current"`.

dataSources: EPA AWIA Reporting Portal submissions (`index=compliance sourcetype="epa:awia"`), utility GRC (`snow:grc_awia`). Lookup `awia_pws_registry_lookup` (pwsid, system_name, state, population_served, tier, rra_completed_at, rra_certification_submitted_at, certification_letter_url, next_rra_due_at, manager, manager_email).
app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
spl:
| inputlookup awia_pws_registry_lookup
| where population_served > 3300
| eval tier = case(
    population_served >= 100000, "tier1_100k_plus",
    population_served >= 50000, "tier2_50k_99k",
    population_served > 3300, "tier3_3301_49999",
    1=1, "out_of_scope")
| eval days_since_rra = if(isnotnull(rra_completed_at), round((now() - rra_completed_at) / 86400, 0), null())
| eval days_to_due = if(isnotnull(next_rra_due_at), round((next_rra_due_at - now()) / 86400, 0), null())
| eval rra_status = case(
    isnull(rra_completed_at), "never_completed",
    isnull(rra_certification_submitted_at), "certification_missing",
    days_to_due < 0, "overdue",
    days_to_due <= 90, "approaching_due",
    1=1, "current")
| table pwsid system_name population_served tier rra_completed_at days_since_rra rra_certification_submitted_at next_rra_due_at days_to_due rra_status certification_letter_url
description: Verifies every CWS subject to AWIA s1433 has a current RRA, a submitted EPA certification, and a tracked 5-year update cycle. Flags `overdue`, `approaching_due`, and `certification_missing` status.
value: AWIA RRA non-compliance is the most-cited finding in EPA AWIA audit work since 2020. The 5-year update cycle is binary: either the new RRA is complete and certified by the due date or it isn't. This UC keeps the cadence visible to the Compliance Officer.
implementation: (1) Maintain `awia_pws_registry_lookup` from the utility GRC, with one row per PWS (each public water system is a separate certification). (2) Populate `next_rra_due_at = rra_completed_at + 5 years`. (3) Schedule UC-22.53.1 daily; route `overdue` and `never_completed` to notable severity=critical. (4) Archive every row.
visualization: (Panel 1) Per-system RAG status. (Panel 2) Aging funnel. (Panel 3) Tier-distribution map. (Panel 4) Certification-letter inventory.

references.title: Safe Drinking Water Act, 42 U.S.C. § 300i-2 (Section 1433, as amended by AWIA Section 2013)
EPA Memorandum — AWIA s2013 Compliance Schedule

knownFalsePositives:
1. **New system commissioning** — a CWS commissioned within the last year may not yet have an RRA; suppression via `commissioning_date` tolerance up to 6 months.

2. **Mid-cycle utility merger** — a merger may consolidate two PWSIDs; legacy RRA inheritance via documented merger record.

3. **EPA extension** — EPA may grant a short extension in writing; recorded in `awia_extension_lookup`.

4. **System ownership change** — the new owner inherits the RRA cycle; updates lookup.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `awia_22_53_1_exceptions`; max grant 90 days with Compliance Officer sign-off.
mitreAttack: N/A (compliance-cadence workflow — measures AWIA s1433 RRA evidence currency, not adversary detection)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- UC-22.44.1 (US-Federal water sector compliance baseline) deployed.
- ES, ServiceNow add-on.
- `index=compliance`, `index=audit_evidence`.
- Lookup `awia_pws_registry_lookup` columns: `pwsid` (PWS identifier), `system_name`, `state`, `population_served`, `tier`, `rra_completed_at` (epoch), `rra_certification_submitted_at`, `certification_letter_url`, `next_rra_due_at` (= rra_completed_at + 5y), `manager`, `manager_email`.
- For Tier 1 (>=100,000 served) — first deadline 31 March 2020; for Tier 2 (>=50,000 served) — 31 December 2020; for Tier 3 (>3,300 served) — 30 June 2021.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

The registry is maintained by the utility's Compliance Officer; for very large utilities use SDWIS integration.

```spl
index=compliance sourcetype=epa:awia event_type=certification earliest=-1d@d
| dedup pwsid sortby - _time
| eval next_rra_due_at = rra_completed_at + (5*365*86400)
| outputlookup awia_pws_registry_lookup
```

## Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Schedule daily:

```ini
[UC-22.53.1: AWIA s1433 RRA currency]
cron_schedule = 0 6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.53.1,reg=AWIA,clause=AWIA-s1433a
action.notable.param.rule_title = AWIA s1433 RRA overdue
action.notable.param.security_domain = audit
action.notable.param.severity = critical
alert.severity = 5
```

Notable severity by `rra_status`: `overdue` / `never_completed` → critical; `certification_missing` → high; `approaching_due` → medium digest.

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a)** Positive control: backdate `rra_completed_at` to 5 years 2 months; UC flags `overdue`.

**(b)** Negative control: refresh RRA + certification; UC clears.

**(c)** Coverage: every PWS the utility operates has at least one row.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

**Dashboard**: `AWIA — RRA Currency`.

**Runbook for `approaching_due`** (owner: Compliance Officer, severity P2):

1. Within 90 days: contract or staff the RRA refresh; engage AWWA J100 methodology (UC-22.53.20).

**Runbook for `overdue`** (owner: Compliance Officer + General Manager, severity P1):

1. Within 30 days: complete RRA and submit certification.
2. Self-disclose to EPA Region; document the gap.
3. Within 60 days: produce a corrective-action plan.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

**SDWIS lag**: SDWIS may not reflect the latest certification; rely on the utility's own GRC.

**Multi-state systems**: each PWSID has its own deadline; ensure separate rows.

**AWIA-specific failure modes**:

- **Population-tier crossover**: a system that grew above a tier threshold mid-cycle must complete the RRA per the new tier's content expectations on the next refresh.
- **Joint RRA (interconnected systems)**: some utilities operate interconnected systems with a shared RRA; ensure each PWSID still has its own certification record.
- **Tribal water systems**: subject to AWIA but reporting routes through EPA Region 10 / 9; ensure portal use matches.
- **PFAS / emerging-contaminant overlay**: RRA refresh under PFAS NPDWR requires assessment of PFAS-related risk; cross-reference.
grandmaExplanation: If we run a community water system with more than 3,300 customers, the law makes us write a 'risk and resilience' check-up of our systems and submit a certificate to the EPA. We must redo it every 5 years. We watch the clock; if the certificate is overdue or coming due, the bell rings.
